playground.title: Monday Rover (Raspberry Pi)
playground.cardDescription: Raspberry Pi robotics exploration with computer vision.
playground.description:
Monday Rover is a robotics exploration project built to iterate quickly on perception + control loops.

  • Computer vision-assisted behaviors (prototype stage)
  • Hardware bring-up, sensor/actuator integration, and debugging on-device
  • Emphasis on tight feedback cycles and observability while experimenting

playground.title: Generative Video Platform
playground.cardDescription: Exploring generative AI for video workflows, from UX to pipeline shape.
playground.description:
Generative Video Platform (GVP) explores how generative systems fit into real video workflows—where latency, repeatability, and UX matter.

  • Product-shaped prototyping: iterate on UI, prompts, and outputs together
  • Pipeline thinking: inputs → generation → review → publish
  • Focus on composability and clear system boundaries as it evolves

portfolio.title: Apptio (an IBM company)
portfolio.cardDescription: Enterprise TBM / IT financial management SaaS — data-intensive, customer-critical.
portfolio.description:
Apptio (IBM) — a platform modernization project for financial data pipelines where correctness and traceability were mandatory.

  • Kubernetes/Java workloads were re-architected onto EMR Spark with Scala to improve throughput and cost efficiency
  • A reconciliation layer compared legacy and modernized outputs in parallel before each cutover step
  • Infrastructure and runtime paths were standardized to improve operability, observability, and cost control across data jobs
  • An incremental rollout model preserved planning and cost-reporting accuracy throughout the migration

portfolio.title: Jumpcloud
portfolio.cardDescription: Cloud directory + device management — identity and access at org scale.
portfolio.description:
JumpCloud — identity and device-management platform work for distributed organizations operating at enterprise scale.

  • Core workflows were designed around security, reliability, and predictable behavior under organizational load
  • API and integration surfaces prioritized operational correctness and safe backward-compatible evolution
  • Delivery patterns emphasized guardrails, progressive rollout, and clear failure boundaries

portfolio.title: Instant Ink (HP)
portfolio.cardDescription: Subscription commerce + fulfillment systems for high-volume consumer services.
portfolio.description:
Instant Ink (HP) — global scaling efforts that evolved subscription and fulfillment systems from early scale to millions of subscribers.

  • The Ruby on Rails monolith was decomposed into cloud-native services and data pipelines
  • Domain-Driven Design established clearer service boundaries across subscription and fulfillment workflows
  • Cross-team TDD practices helped maintain reliability and service stability during rapid expansion

portfolio.title: AT&T
portfolio.cardDescription: TQL: a SQL-like query language for ticket search and operational analytics.
portfolio.description:
AT&T — TQL project: a SQL-like domain language for ticket search and operational analytics workflows.

  • The language model balanced expressive queries with predictable semantics and bounded execution behavior
  • Query flows were shaped around real support operations, including filters, search, and drill-down analytics
  • Grammar, validation, and test coverage were structured for maintainability and debuggable execution paths

portfolio.title: Master's in Computer Science
portfolio.cardDescription: M.S. CS focus: machine learning, data science, and software engineering practice.
portfolio.description:
Master's in Computer Science (MIU) — applied graduate work spanning machine learning, data science, and software engineering delivery discipline.

  • Projects emphasized turning ML/data foundations into production-oriented engineering decisions
  • Algorithmic and systems thinking were applied to solution design under practical constraints
  • SDLC rigor and implementation quality were treated as first-class outcomes, not afterthoughts

portfolio.title: Sunrise Resorts & Cruises
portfolio.cardDescription: Infra + ops: datacenters, VPN/networking, and internal tooling.
portfolio.description:
Sunrise Resorts & Cruises — infrastructure and operations initiatives supporting business-critical hospitality systems.

  • Datacenter and VPN/network architecture supported always-on operational workflows across sites
  • Internal tooling improved asset tracking and day-to-day operational visibility
  • Execution favored reliability, security controls, and pragmatic automation over unnecessary complexity

portfolio.title: Campus Party Milenio (Spain)
portfolio.cardDescription: Community + collaboration: technical exchange with an international builder community.
portfolio.description:
Campus Party Milenio (Spain) — international technical collaboration focused on rapid experimentation and cross-discipline exchange.

  • Projects and demos were developed in builder-heavy environments with fast feedback loops
  • Emerging technology ideas were pressure-tested through hands-on iteration and peer review
  • The working model emphasized shipping early, learning publicly, and refining quickly

portfolio.title: 5d-agency (SWI)
portfolio.cardDescription: Client delivery across web/mobile, AR, and Unity-powered experiences.
portfolio.description:
5d-agency (SWI) — multi-platform client delivery across web, mobile, AR, and Unity-based interactive products.

  • Delivery flows balanced creative product goals with fixed timelines and implementation constraints
  • Interactive and AR experiences were engineered for usability, performance, and maintainability
  • Execution cadence prioritized shipping, stabilization, and iterative quality improvements

portfolio.title: Early Startups
portfolio.cardDescription: Early-stage work across web apps, embedded Linux, and research tooling.
portfolio.description:
Early startups — cross-domain product engineering across ad-tech, embedded Linux, and research-oriented web tooling.

  • DDA Advertising: ad-tech and web systems focused on rapid product iteration
  • OIG OS: Linux-based embedded systems under hardware and platform constraints
  • QREO: web applications and research tooling designed for exploratory workflows
